function.description: Prettify or minify a JSON document (also validates it) Returns: The reformatted JSON document
function.name: json_format

minify.description: Emit minified JSON instead of pretty-printed

function.description: Run a jq filter on JSON input (full jq language via the jaq engine) Returns: The filter's output value(s)
function.name: json_query

function.description: Structural diff of two JSON documents as an RFC 6902 patch Returns: RFC 6902 patch array (op/path/value entries)
function.name: json_diff

files.description: Treat the two arguments as file paths

new.description: New JSON document, or a file path with --files

old.description: Old JSON document, or a file path with --files

function.description: Deep-merge a JSON merge patch into a base document (RFC 7396) Returns: The merged document
function.name: json_merge

base.description: Base JSON document, or a file path with --files

files.description: Treat the two arguments as file paths

patch.description: Merge patch JSON, or a file path with --files

function.description: Flatten nested JSON to dot-notation keys, or rebuild nesting with --nestify Returns: Flat object with dot-notation keys (or nested JSON with --nestify)
function.name: json_flatten

nestify.description: Rebuild nested JSON from flattened keys

function.description: Escape text as a JSON/code string literal, or unescape with --unescape Returns: The escaped literal (quoted) or the unescaped text
function.name: json_escape

unescape.description: Unescape a quoted string literal instead

function.description: Convert structured data between JSON, YAML, TOML, XML and CSV Returns: The converted document in the target format
function.name: data_convert

from.description: Source format: json | yaml | toml | xml | csv

to.description: Target format: json | yaml | toml | xml | csv

function.description: Validate JSON, YAML, TOML or XML syntax Returns: {"valid": true, "format": ...} on success; parse error on stderr otherwise
function.name: data_lint

function.description: Encode text to Base64 (--url-safe for the URL alphabet without padding) Returns: Base64 string, single line
function.name: encode_base64

function.description: Encode text to a lowercase hex string Returns: Hex string, single line
function.name: encode_hex

function.description: Gzip-compress text and emit it as Base64 Returns: Base64 of the gzip-compressed bytes, single line
function.name: encode_gzip

function.description: Convert an internationalized domain name to ASCII punycode (IDNA) Returns: The xn-- encoded domain
function.name: encode_punycode

function.description: Escape text for safe embedding in HTML (quotes included) Returns: HTML-escaped text
function.name: encode_html

function.description: Decode Base64 to text (standard and URL-safe alphabets both accepted) Returns: The decoded text
function.name: decode_base64

function.description: Decode a hex string to text (0x prefix and whitespace tolerated) Returns: The decoded text
function.name: decode_hex

function.description: Decompress Base64-encoded gzip data back to text Returns: The decompressed text
function.name: decode_gzip

function.description: Convert an ASCII punycode domain back to unicode Returns: The unicode domain
function.name: decode_punycode

function.description: Decode HTML entities back to plain text Returns: The decoded text
function.name: decode_html

function.description: Percent-encode text for safe use in a URL component Returns: Percent-encoded string, single line
function.name: url_encode

function.description: Decode a percent-encoded URL component Returns: The decoded text
function.name: url_decode

function.description: Parse a URL into scheme, host, port, path, query params, fragment and credentials Returns: Object with scheme, username, password, host, port, path, query, params (decoded key/value map) and fragment
function.name: url_parse

function.description: Build a URL from a base, path, query parameters and fragment with correct encoding Returns: The assembled URL, single line
function.name: url_build

param.description: Query parameter key=value (repeatable, values get percent-encoded)

function.description: Decode a JWT's header and payload WITHOUT verifying the signature Returns: Object with header, payload, signature (raw base64url) and expired (true/false, or null when no exp claim)
function.name: jwt_decode

function.description: Hash text or a file (--file PATH) with md5, sha1, sha224, sha256, sha384 or sha512 (hex digest) Returns: Lowercase hex digest, single line
function.name: crypto_hash

algo.description: Algorithm: md5 | sha1 | sha224 | sha256 | sha384 | sha512 (default: sha256)

file.description: Hash the raw bytes of this file instead of text input

function.description: HMAC signature of text with a secret key (md5, sha1, sha256, sha512) Returns: Lowercase hex signature, single line
function.name: crypto_hmac

algo.description: Algorithm: md5 | sha1 | sha256 | sha512 (default: sha256)

key.description: Secret key as a literal argument (fine for test values; real secrets should use --key-env or --key-file so they never appear in argv)

key_env.description: Read the key from this environment variable

key_file.description: Read the key from a file (one trailing newline stripped)

function.description: Hash a password with bcrypt (salted, cost-configurable) Returns: bcrypt hash string ($2b$...), single line
function.name: crypto_bcrypt_hash

function.description: Check a password against an existing bcrypt hash Returns: {"valid": true|false}; exit code 2 when false
function.name: crypto_bcrypt_verify

hash.description: Existing bcrypt hash to check against

function.description: Generate or verify a TOTP code (RFC 6238: SHA1, 6 digits, 30s period) Returns: {code, seconds_remaining} when generating; {valid} when checking
function.name: crypto_otp

check.description: Verify this code instead of generating one (exit 2 on mismatch)

secret.description: Base32 secret as a literal (real secrets should use --secret-env)

secret_env.description: Read the Base32 secret from this environment variable

function.description: Generate UUIDs (v4 random, v7 time-ordered, nil) Returns: One UUID per line, lowercase hyphenated
function.name: generate_uuid

count.description: Number of UUIDs to generate (one per line) (default: 1)

function.description: Generate ULIDs (time-ordered, lexicographically sortable identifiers) Returns: One 26-character Crockford-Base32 ULID per line
function.name: generate_ulid

count.description: Number of ULIDs to generate (one per line) (default: 1)

function.description: Generate Nano IDs (URL-safe random identifiers, default length 21) Returns: One Nano ID per line (A-Za-z0-9_-)
function.name: generate_nanoid

count.description: Number of IDs to generate (one per line) (default: 1)

function.description: Generate cryptographically random token strings (configurable charset) Returns: One random token per line
function.name: generate_token

count.description: Number of tokens to generate (one per line) (default: 1)

function.description: Convert a string between naming cases (camel, pascal, snake, constant, kebab, train, title, dot, path, lower, upper) Returns: The re-cased string, single line
function.name: text_case

to.description: Target case: camel | pascal | snake | constant | kebab | train | title | dot | path | lower | upper

function.description: Turn any string into a URL-safe slug (ASCII, lowercase, hyphen-separated) Returns: The slug, single line
function.name: text_slugify

function.description: Count bytes, characters (graphemes), words and lines of a text Returns: {bytes, characters, characters_without_whitespace, words, lines, longest_line_characters}
function.name: text_stats

function.description: Similarity metrics between two strings: Levenshtein, normalized Levenshtein, Jaro-Winkler Returns: {levenshtein, normalized_levenshtein, jaro_winkler, equal}
function.name: text_distance

function.description: Mask sensitive data in text: emails, IPv4s, JWTs, bearer tokens, AWS keys, card numbers, long hex secrets Returns: {masked: the sanitized text, found: counts per pattern type}
function.name: text_mask

function.description: Unified diff between two texts, or two files with --files Returns: Unified diff (--- old / +++ new hunks); empty when inputs are equal
function.name: text_diff

files.description: Treat the two arguments as file paths

new.description: New text, or a file path with --files

old.description: Old text, or a file path with --files

function.description: Test a regular expression against text; reports all matches with positions and named groups Returns: Object with pattern, is_match, match_count and matches (each with match, start, end byte offsets, and groups keyed by name or index)
function.name: regex_test

input.description: Text to test (reads stdin if omitted)

function.description: Current time as unix seconds/ms, ISO 8601 (UTC), RFC 2822 and local time Returns: Object with unix, unix_ms, iso8601 (UTC), rfc2822 and local fields
function.name: time_now

function.description: Convert a timestamp (unix s/ms, RFC 3339, RFC 2822) into all common formats Returns: Object with unix, unix_ms, iso8601 (UTC), rfc2822 and local fields
function.name: time_convert

input.description: Timestamp or date string (reads stdin if omitted)

function.description: Add or subtract a duration (e.g. '2h 30m', '1w 2d') from a timestamp Returns: {base, operation, duration_seconds, result: {unix, iso8601, ...}}
function.name: time_duration

from.description: Base timestamp: unix s/ms, RFC 3339 or RFC 2822 (defaults to now)

function.description: Convert a timestamp into a target IANA timezone (DST-aware) Returns: {utc, timezone, local, offset, abbreviation}
function.name: time_timezone

input.description: Timestamp: unix s/ms, RFC 3339 or RFC 2822 (defaults to now)

function.description: Validate a crontab expression and list its next run times (UTC) Returns: Object with expression, normalized (6-field form), valid, and next (array of RFC 3339 UTC timestamps)
function.name: time_cron

count.description: Number of upcoming occurrences to list (default: 5)

expression.description: Cron expression, 5-field classic or 6/7-field with seconds/years

function.description: Build an HTTP Basic Auth Authorization header value from username and password Returns: The header value: 'Basic <base64(user:pass)>'
function.name: http_basic_auth

function.description: Parse a user-agent string into browser, version, OS and device category Returns: Object with name, category (pc/smartphone/crawler/...), browser_type, version, os, os_version, vendor
function.name: http_user_agent

function.description: Look up the MIME type for a file name/extension, or extensions for a MIME type Returns: {input, mime} or {mime, extensions}
function.name: http_mime

input.description: A file name/extension (e.g. photo.avif) or a MIME type (e.g. image/png)

function.description: Parse any CSS color (hex, rgb(), hsl(), named) and show hex, rgb, hsl and WCAG luminance Returns: {hex, rgb, hsl, luminance (WCAG relative luminance 0-1)}
function.name: color_parse

input.description: Any CSS color: hex, rgb(), hsl(), named... (reads stdin if omitted)

function.description: WCAG 2.x contrast ratio between two CSS colors, with AA/AAA verdicts Returns: {ratio, aa_normal_text, aa_large_text, aaa_normal_text, aaa_large_text}
function.name: color_contrast

function.description: Render Markdown (GitHub-flavored: tables, strikethrough, task lists, autolinks) to HTML Returns: The rendered HTML fragment
function.name: markdown_to_html

function.description: Convert HTML to Markdown Returns: The Markdown document
function.name: markdown_from_html

function.description: Analyze an IPv4/IPv6 CIDR block: network, broadcast, netmask, usable host range Returns: IPv4: {cidr, network, broadcast, netmask, wildcard, prefix, addresses, usable_hosts, first_usable, last_usable}; IPv6: {cidr, network, last, prefix, addresses}
function.name: network_subnet

function.description: First and last address (and count) of a CIDR block Returns: {cidr, first, last, addresses}
function.name: network_cidr_to_range

function.description: Smallest set of CIDR blocks exactly covering an IP range Returns: {cidrs: [...]}
function.name: network_range_to_cidr

function.description: Check whether an IP address or CIDR is contained in a CIDR block Returns: {outer, inner, contains}; exit 2 when contains is false
function.name: network_cidr_contains

inner.description: IP address or CIDR to test

function.description: Convert an IP address between dotted, integer, hex and binary representations Returns: IPv4: {dotted, decimal, hex, binary, ipv6_mapped}; IPv6: {compressed, expanded, decimal, hex}
function.name: network_ip

input.description: IPv4/IPv6 address, or an integer (interpreted as IPv4)

function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression exactly (functions like sin, cos, sqrt, ln supported) Returns: The numeric result, single line (integers print without decimals)
function.name: math_calc

function.description: Bitwise operations (and, or, xor, not, shl, shr) on integers with 0x/0b/0o prefixes Returns: Result as {decimal, hex, binary, octal}
function.name: math_bitwise

b.description: Second operand (shift amount for shl/shr; omitted for not)

op.description: Operation: and | or | xor | not | shl | shr

function.description: Convert an integer between numeral bases 2-36 Returns: The number in the target base (lowercase digits), single line
function.name: math_number_base

function.description: Convert chmod permissions between octal (755, 4755) and symbolic (rwxr-xr-x) notation Returns: {octal, symbolic, command}
function.name: unix_chmod
